findings.reachability: Any authenticated user with the 'edit_posts' capability (Contributor and above) can reach handle_ajax_request via wp_ajax_ hook after passing a valid nonce.
findings.justification: Raw $_REQUEST['selected_option'] and $_REQUEST['additional_info'] are copied verbatim into the payload array and transmitted to WPDesk's external tracking server via send_payload() with no sanitization, type-checking, or length restriction, allowing a Contributor to inject arbitrary data into WPDesk's telemetry pipeline.

detection_pattern.section_name: Unsanitized input forwarded to external endpoint
detection_pattern.description: Catches AJAX or form handlers that read raw superglobal values and forward them externally or store them without sanitization; the anti_pattern confirms a sanitization call is present near the input read.

findings.reachability: Same as above — any Contributor-level authenticated user.
findings.justification: prepare_payload($_REQUEST) is called twice on consecutive lines: once to assign $payload (line 73) and again as the argument to send_payload_to_wpdesk() (line 74), causing apply_filters('wpdesk_tracker_user_feedback_data', ...) to fire twice and sending a second HTTP request to the external endpoint, which could double-count feedback events in WPDesk's analytics.

detection_pattern.section_name: Duplicate function call causing double external submission
detection_pattern.description: Flags functions named prepare_payload (or similar) called more than once in the same handler context, which may indicate accidental double-submission to external services.

findings.reachability: Any logged-in user with the 'edit_posts' capability, which includes Contributors — the lowest non-subscriber role in WordPress.
findings.justification: The capability gate current_user_can('edit_posts') allows Contributors to submit feedback telemetry to an external WPDesk server; a more appropriate capability would be 'manage_options' (admin) or 'activate_plugins' since this is a plugin-management telemetry feature, not a content-editing feature.

detection_pattern.section_name: Weak capability: edit_posts used for admin/telemetry action
detection_pattern.description: Detects handlers that gate privileged or administrative operations only on edit_posts (a Contributor-level capability) rather than a properly scoped admin capability.
